name: accessibility
tagline: Enforces accessibility requirements (help dialog, accessible view, verbosity setting, ARIA, keyboard nav) for new VS Code UI contributions.
verdict: Thorough, technically precise coding-pattern guide with concrete TypeScript skeletons, a completion checklist, and clear file pointers into the target codebase; no security concerns. Weak spots: an aggressive 'apply by default even when not asked' trigger with no stated exclusions, and no versioning signal.

highlights.summary: No 'NOT for'/exclusion text anywhere, despite line 9's broad 'use by default... even when accessibility is not named in the prompt.'
Line 1: no version field in frontmatter and no changelog/dated revision notes anywhere in the file.
